name: manual_973192
claim: Before a structural HTML change (lifting heading levels, renaming elements, changing tag names), grep the stylesheet for selectors that target the old element — update both HTML and CSS in the same pass.

decisions.notes: On the WCAG pass for Sea Tree I ran 'replace_all <h4 → <h3' without checking styles.css, which targets .amenity-card h4, .bedroom-info h4, .footer h4, etc. The user reverted the HTML change to save styling. Loss = one round-trip + a partial commit that had to be unwound. Even simple find-replace on an HTML tag name is not safe without a CSS selector audit.

rationale: On the WCAG pass for Sea Tree I ran 'replace_all <h4 → <h3' without checking styles.css, which targets .amenity-card h4, .bedroom-info h4, .footer h4, etc. The user reverted the HTML change to save styling. Loss = one round-trip + a partial commit that had to be unwound. Even simple find-replace on an HTML tag name is not safe without a CSS selector audit.
